Off Calendar May-27-2014 Continued To Mar-11-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DAVID LOWE, ESQ., 415.434.9800, FOR PLAINTIFF GERALD TREBESCH.
ROBIN SAMUEL, ESQ., 310.785.4600, FOR DEFENDANTS FALL LINE CAPITAL, LLC, ERIC O BRIEN AND CLAY MITCHELL.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs FALL LINE CAPITAL, LLC, ERIC O BRIEN and CLAY MITCHELL's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings is granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, eleventh, twelfth, and thirteenth causes of action. These causes of action are "arising out of, in connection with, or relating to" the Limited Liability Company Agreement (the "GP Agreement"). (Declaration of Eric O'Brien, Ex. A, par. 9.2.) Although Fall Line GP is not a named defendant in this matter, Plaintiff seeks to recover under the GP Agreement, and therefore he is precluded from repudiating the arbitration clause in the contract entered into with Fall Line GP. (Rowe v. Excline (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 1276, 1287.) The GP Agreement is not unconscionable. Denied as to the sixth, seventh, eighth, ninth, and tenth causes of action. These causes of action arise from Plaintiff's employment with Fall Line and do not seek damages under the GP Agreement. The arbitration provision in the TriNet Agreement does not govern these claims. The TriNet Agreement defines covered disputes as those arising out of or relating to Plaintiff's employment with TriNet. (O'Brien Decl., Ex. B, sec. 9(a).) The TriNet Agreement states that TriNet is Plaintiff's co-employer for administrative purposes. (Id. at sec. 1.) Plaintiff's claims do not arise or relate to administrative issues. Alternatively, the scope of the arbitration provision in the TriNet Agreement is ambiguous. "A contract may be explained by reference to the circumstances under which it was made, and the matter to which it relates." (Civ. Code 1647.) The evidence establishes that Defendants informed Plaintiff that the TriNet Agreement does not govern the terms of Plaintiff's employment with Fall Line. (Declaration of Gerald Trebesch, Ex. A.) The litigation in this Court is stayed pending the outcome of arbitration. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Continued To May-20-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearance. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FALL LINE CAPITAL, LLC, ERIC O BRIEN, and CLAY MITCHELL'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings is continued on the Court's motion to May 20, 2014. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
